methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Mazama is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Sriram Vasantharajan that has raised $30M+ to date. The Climate & Energy sector comprises 1 public comparable (LNZA) and 8 private peers including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen. However, peer-median valuation is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow Climate & Energy segment—only 0 peers met stage and tier filters despite 9 total sector companies in the dataset.

Stage-progression analysis, applied here as the sole triangulation method, uses Mazama's $30M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage step-ups, yielding $60M. This method is applicable given the company's clear stage positioning and capital history, though the multiplier reflects generic stage progression rather than company-specific metrics.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $51M (low) to $69M (high), with a median of $60M. This narrow band reflects reliance on a single methodology. Key uncertainties include the lack of granular peer data at this stage in Climate & Energy, absence of revenue or unit economics in the provided dataset, and sensitivity to the 2.0x multiplier assumption. A fuller valuation would benefit from peer multiples once additional same-stage private comps become available.
